_notes: Source: AU Core Location (Australian realm). Target: US Core Location (US realm). Key differences: AU Core has an error constraint (au-core-loc-01) absent in US Core; AU Core marks type and physicalType as MS while US Core does not; US Core makes name mandatory (min=1) while AU Core has it optional MS; US Core adds telecom MS and address sub-element constraints (line/city/state/postalCode) with US-state binding; both have address and managingOrganization MS; managingOrganization reference targets differ; US Core adds status MS absent in AU Core.

drifts.description: Source constraint au-core-loc-01(error) removed in target: AU Core defines an error-level constraint on Location (requiring fields when certain conditions are met); US Core Location has no equivalent root constraint.

identifier type profile changed: source allows AU-specific identifier profiles (au-locationspecificpracticenumber, au-natasitenumber) on identifier elements; target US Core uses only the base Identifier type. AU-specific identifier profiles removed.
status MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds status[mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain status in its differential. MS added on optional element.
name min cardinality strictly increased: source [0..1, mustSupport=true] → target [min=1, mustSupport=true]. Min increased from 0 to 1; name becomes mandatory.
telecom MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds telecom[mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain telecom in its differential. MS added on optional element.
type MustSupport removed on optional element: source AU Core marks type[mustSupport=true]; target US Core does not constrain type in its differential. MS removed on optional element.

type binding changed: source extensible(http://terminology.hl7.org.au/ValueSet/v3-ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType-extended) [AU extended service delivery location VS] → target extensible(http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v3-ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType) [R4 base VS]. Same binding strength, AU-extended VS replaced.

address.line MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds address.line[max=*, mustSupport=true]; source AU Core marks address as MS but does not constrain address sub-elements. MS added on optional element.

address type profile changed: source AU Core uses address with au-address profile (http://hl7.org.au/fhir/StructureDefinition/au-address); target US Core uses the base Address type. AU-specific address profile removed.
address.city MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds address.city[mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain address.city. MS added on optional element.
address.state MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds address.state[mustSupport=true]; source does not constrain address.state. MS added on optional element.

address.state new binding added in target: US Core adds extensible(http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/ValueSet/us-core-usps-state) [US postal state codes]; source has no binding on address.state. New US realm-specific binding applied.
address.postalCode MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds address.postalCode[mustSupport=true]; source does not constrain address.postalCode. MS added on optional element.

physicalType MustSupport removed on optional element: source AU Core marks physicalType[mustSupport=true]; target US Core does not constrain physicalType in its differential. MS removed on optional element.

physicalType binding changed: source preferred(http://terminology.hl7.org.au/ValueSet/location-physical-type-extended) [AU extended location physical type VS] → target example(http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/location-physical-type) [R4 base VS]. Binding strength weakened from preferred to example and AU-extended VS replaced.

managingOrganization reference target changed: source constrains to au-core-organization (http://hl7.org.au/fhir/core/StructureDefinition/au-core-organization) → target constrains to us-core-organization (http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/StructureDefinition/us-core-organization). Different realm-specific organization profiles.
